name: Fernando Mendoza
notes: Heisman Trophy winner who elevated Indiana's program to new heights. Elite processor with outstanding accuracy and pocket presence.

name: Arvell Reese
notes: LB/EDGE hybrid who racked up 69 tackles, 10 TFL, and 6.5 sacks. Versatile enough to play standing up or with a hand in the dirt.

name: Jeremiyah Love
notes: Explosive three-down back with elite burst and receiving ability out of the backfield. Rare combination of power and speed.

name: Caleb Downs
notes: Instinctive safety with elite range and the versatility to play single-high or in the box. One of the smartest defensive players in this class.

name: Sonny Styles
notes: 6'4", 240 lbs with a 4.46 40-yard dash and 9.99 RAS — freakish athleticism for the position. Dominant run defender and capable pass rusher.

name: Rueben Bain Jr.
notes: ACC Defensive Player of the Year in 2025 with 15.5 TFL and 9.5 sacks. Explosive first step and relentless pursuit make him a nightmare for tackles.

name: David Bailey
notes: FBS-leading 14.5 sacks in 2025 with a 4.50 40. Relentless motor and elite bend around the edge make him a Day 1 starter.

name: Mansoor Delane
notes: Long, physical corner with outstanding ball skills developed in the SEC. Projects as a true lockdown outside corner at the next level.

name: Carnell Tate
notes: 51 catches for 875 yards and 9 TDs in 2025. Silky route runner with elite hands and the football IQ to win at all three levels.

name: Francis Mauigoa
notes: Consensus #1 OT at 6'6", 319 lbs with outstanding length and anchor. Dominant run blocker who is ascending as a pass protector.

name: Spencer Fano
notes: Technically refined guard prospect out of Utah's NFL pipeline. Dominant at the point of attack with elite hand fighting ability.

name: Jermod McCoy
notes: Competitive SEC corner who excels in press-man coverage. Quick-twitch athlete with the physicality to match up with big receivers.

name: Makai Lemon
notes: Biletnikoff Award winner with 1,156 yards and 11 TDs. Exceptional after the catch with burst that creates big plays in any scheme.

name: Olaivavega Ioane
notes: Versatile interior lineman capable of playing center or guard at the next level. Outstanding football intelligence and technique.

name: Jordyn Tyson
notes: Big-play receiver with elite speed and the ability to take the top off a defense. Needs to improve route precision but the upside is enormous.

name: Kenyon Sadiq
notes: 6'3", 250 lbs with a 4.39 40 and 11'1" broad jump — elite athlete for the position. Dangerous mismatch weapon in the passing game.

name: Monroe Freeling
notes: Experienced Georgia tackle who protected elite QB prospects for three seasons. Smart, technically sound with good length and anchor.

name: Dillon Thieneman
notes: Instinctive safety from Oregon who reads the game at an elite level. Reliable tackler who fits well in both deep zone and Cover 1 looks.

name: Kadyn Proctor
notes: Alabama-developed tackle with elite size and the fundamentals to start from Day 1. Excels in gap schemes as a powerful run blocker.

name: Avieon Terrell
notes: Athletic Clemson corner with the length and ball skills to match up outside. Quick hip flip and fluid footwork make him a natural man-coverage corner.

name: Keldric Faulk
notes: Explosive Auburn EDGE with elite get-off and the burst to beat tackles around the corner. Ascending prospect with significant upside.

name: Emmanuel McNeil-Warren
notes: G5 standout who dominated with elite athleticism and ball-hawking instincts. High ceiling prospect who impressed at every pre-draft evaluation.

name: Akheem Mesidor
notes: Freakish athlete along the Miami defensive front with elite first-step quickness. Can win with speed or power and has upside as a hand-in-dirt end.

name: Peter Woods
notes: Explosive Clemson interior lineman with a relentless motor. Projects as a 3-technique in a 4-3 scheme with the pass-rush upside to be a starter.

name: Omar Cooper Jr.
notes: Productive Heisman-supporting receiver who earned respect with precision route running and reliable hands in big moments.

name: Caleb Lomu
notes: Physically imposing Utah tackle with excellent athleticism and upside. Long arms and good feet project well to the NFL as a starting right tackle.

name: KC Concepcion
notes: Reliable possession receiver with outstanding hands and route-running polish. Consistent producer who fits well as a starter in multiple offensive systems.

name: Kayden McDonald
notes: Versatile interior defender capable of lining up at 3-tech or nose. Consistent pass-rush production from the interior with a quick get-off.

name: Denzel Boston
notes: Big receiver with outstanding catch radius and YAC ability after the catch. Physically dominant at the catch point and tough to bring down.

name: Blake Miller
notes: Athletic Clemson tackle with excellent footwork in pass protection. Has the tools to develop into a starting right tackle immediately.

name: CJ Allen
notes: AP All-American with 88 tackles and 8 TFL in 2025. Thumper linebacker who excels in run defense and has improved dramatically in coverage.

name: Ty Simpson
notes: 3,567 yards and 28 TDs with just 5 INTs at Alabama. Game manager who maximizes efficiency within structure; ceiling may be a solid backup or bridge starter.

name: T.J. Parker
notes: Clemson's top defensive player with a refined pass-rush repertoire. Effective counter moves and relentless effort make him a complete EDGE.

name: Cashius Howell
notes: SEC-tested EDGE rusher with a developing array of pass-rush moves. High motor player who consistently creates pressure despite modest sack totals.

name: Colton Hood
notes: Savvy Tennessee corner with great anticipation and ball production. Slightly undersized but makes up for it with elite football instincts.

name: Caleb Banks
notes: Powerful Gators nose tackle who anchors the middle of the defense. Creates disruption with his quick penetration and heavy hands at the point of attack.

name: Max Iheanachor
notes: Length and athleticism stand out for this Arizona State tackle. Raw in technique but the physical tools are undeniable for a starting LT projection.

name: Chris Johnson
notes: G5 standout with elite athleticism and lockdown coverage ability. Showed he could compete with Power 5 talent; pre-draft workouts enhanced his stock.

name: Zion Young
notes: Productive SEC EDGE with a first-step quickness advantage and developing counter moves. Shows the traits to become a reliable starter as a 4-3 end.

name: D'Angelo Ponds
notes: CB prospect from Indiana. Consensus rank #45.

name: Christen Miller
notes: Big-bodied interior DT from Georgia. Dominant against the run. SEC experience.

name: Lee Hunter
notes: Disruptive interior lineman who was a force in the Big 12. Combines good initial quickness with upper-body power to consistently penetrate the backfield.

name: Chase Bisontis
notes: OG prospect from Texas A&M. Consensus rank #58.

name: Brandon Cisse
notes: Physical corner from the SEC with competitive toughness and the length to match up with bigger receivers. Solid tackler who supports the run.

name: Chris Brazzell II
notes: WR prospect from Tennessee. Consensus rank #57.

name: R Mason Thomas
notes: Long Oklahoma EDGE rusher with impressive length and the pass-rush tools to develop into a starter. Needs to add core strength but the tools are there.

name: Anthony Hill Jr.
notes: 4.51 40-yard dash and 9.93 RAS for a linebacker — elite physical profile. Physical downhill thumper who also has the speed to run with backs.

name: Gabe Jacas
notes: Big Ten pass rusher with strong production. Multiple team top-30 visits. Packers target.

name: Jake Golday
notes: LB prospect from Cincinnati. Consensus rank #56.

name: Emmanuel Pregnon
notes: Athletic guard/center versatility. Excels in zone blocking scheme. Seahawks pro day visit.

name: Jadarian Price
notes: Complementary back behind Love at Notre Dame who showed he can carry the load. Slashing style with excellent vision and contact balance.

name: Malachi Lawrence
notes: G5 pass-rush specialist who dominated lesser competition but showed out in big games. Elite get-off and bend with the athleticism to translate to the NFL.

name: Jacob Rodriguez
notes: Rangy linebacker who excels in zone coverage and as a blitzer. High-production player in the Big 12 with good instincts against the run.

name: Germie Bernard
notes: Reliable possession receiver from Alabama with excellent route discipline and hand strength. Fits naturally in the slot with YAC potential after catch.

name: Josiah Trotter
notes: Interior linebacker from Missouri. Big-time hitter between the tackles with great closing speed downhill. Visited Chiefs, Colts, Broncos, Bears. Stephen Holder (ESPN) called him a dream fit for Colts scheme.

name: Eli Stowers
notes: Set a combine record for tight ends with a 45.5" vertical. Freakish athlete who needs to refine his receiving technique but offers massive upside.

name: Zachariah Branch
notes: WR prospect from Georgia. Consensus rank #63.

name: Keionte Scott
notes: Athletic corner from Miami who profiles as a zone specialist at the next level. Long and fluid with the ball skills to play centerfield if needed.

name: Chris Bell
notes: WR prospect from Louisville. Consensus rank #61.

name: Keith Abney II
notes: CB prospect from Arizona State. Consensus rank #53.

name: Antonio Williams
notes: Physical outside receiver from Clemson with excellent contested-catch skills. Will contribute early as a special teams ace while developing as a starter.

name: Keylan Rutledge
notes: Efficient guard/center hybrid who excels in zone-blocking schemes. Above-average athlete for the interior line with dependable pass protection.

name: Max Klare
notes: TE prospect from Ohio State. Consensus rank #70.

name: Derrick Moore
notes: EDGE prospect from Michigan. Consensus rank #60.

name: Treydan Stukes
notes: S prospect from Arizona. Consensus rank #83.

name: Caleb Tiernan
notes: OT prospect from Northwestern. Consensus rank #72.

name: Domonique Orange
notes: DT prospect from Iowa State. Consensus rank #67.

name: Gennings Dunker
notes: OG prospect from Iowa. Consensus rank #66.

name: Elijah Sarratt

name: Connor Lew
notes: C prospect from Auburn. Consensus rank #88.

name: Malachi Fields
notes: WR prospect from Notre Dame. Consensus rank #78.

name: Dani Dennis-Sutton

notes: S prospect from Penn State. Consensus rank #99.

notes: CB prospect from Texas. Consensus rank #71.

name: Garrett Nussmeier
notes: 4,052 yards and 29 TDs at LSU. Aggressive downfield thrower with good arm strength who must clean up his decision-making to succeed at the next level.

name: Darrell Jackson Jr.
notes: Powerful Florida State interior defender who excels against the run. Consistent effort and good anchor make him a reliable starter in a two-gap scheme.

name: Genesis Smith

notes: WR prospect from Connecticut. Consensus rank #106.
